title: Russia Launches 442 Drones and 1 Missile Overnight; 4 Killed Including Strike on Ozernoye Airbase
detail: Russia launched 442 drones and 1 missile against Ukraine overnight on March 28–29, 2026 — the largest single-night drone barrage of the week. Ukraine's Air Force shot down 380 UAVs; 62 penetrated defenses. Odesa was struck again, killing 2 and wounding 13, with port infrastructure hit. One man was killed in a strike on an industrial site in Kryvyi Rih and one person was killed in Poltava region when a residential building and industrial facilities were hit. Ozernoye Airbase in Zhytomyr region was struck by at least 7 Geran-2 (Shahed-136) kamikaze UAVs — the base serves combined combat aviation and personnel training. President Zelensky described the attack as 'pure terror against ordinary civilian life — there was no military purpose whatsoever.' Over the preceding week, Russia fired approximately 3,000 attack drones — nearly double the March weekly average of 1,500–1,750 — alongside 1,450+ guided aerial bombs and 40 missiles of various types.

media.caption: Russia launches 442 drones and missile overnight in continued aerial bombardment

title: Ukraine Strikes Ust-Luga Port 4th Consecutive Night; Destroys 3 Russian Tornado-S MLRS Systems
detail: Ukrainian drones struck Russia's Ust-Luga Baltic export hub for the fourth consecutive night on March 29, triggering fresh fires at the major fertilizer, oil, and coal export facility. Russia's regional governor confirmed new damage. Separately, Ukraine's Unmanned Systems Forces destroyed 3 BM-30 Smerch/Tornado-S multiple rocket launcher systems plus a transport-loader vehicle at a Russian MLRS base overnight March 28–29. Ukrainian drones also struck fuel storage tanks in Russian-occupied Luhansk territory. In Belgorod, Russia, a drone strike killed 1 civilian in the border city of Grayvoron. Russia's air defenses reported intercepting 155 Ukrainian drones in Russian airspace overnight. The cumulative Ust-Luga and Primorsk Baltic port campaign has now reportedly disrupted up to 40% of Russian oil export capacity since commencing on March 23.

media.caption: Ukraine strikes Ust-Luga port for fourth consecutive night

title: Russia Captures Kovsharovka in Kharkiv Oblast; 800 Ukrainian Soldiers Reportedly Encircled; 236 Engagements
detail: Russian forces (1st Guards Tank Army, 'West' Group) captured Kovsharovka in Kharkiv Oblast — a pre-war settlement of approximately 17,960 people, described as the largest urbanized settlement seized in this operation. Russian troops advancing from Kurilovka are consolidating control in the area. Approximately 800 Ukrainian soldiers are reportedly encircled in the Peschanoe–Kupyansk-Uzlovoi–Glushkovka salient, though Ukrainian MoD has not confirmed encirclement. On the Sloviansk axis, Russian 'South' Group is pressing from Nikiforovka toward Rai-Aleksandrovka — a key first-echelon defensive position guarding Sloviansk. On the Zaporizhzhia axis, Russian forces struck 780 times across 41 settlements near Huliaipole. A total of 236 combat engagements were recorded across the frontline on March 29 — the heaviest in the Pokrovsk and Huliaipole sectors.

media.caption: Russia captures Kovsharovka in Kharkiv Oblast amid spring offensive
